qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 派出qa和codex辩论哪些是系统性原因哪些需要修 / /redev --codex 修复全部建议的内容 / 重新加载我们刚刚谈论的内容，禁止加载任何非本cycle的内容
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All 9 retrospective items (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10) have concrete code/spec/doc landings empirically verified. Item 8 correctly remains ACCEPTABLE-DEBT per user 就地部署 directive. NO scope leak from prior task 20260519-161035 (grep all 17 modified files: 0 hits). 4 binding constraints all preserved: (a) docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md zero diff vs d988d4a; (b) d988d4a SHA unchanged; (c) all 14 d988d4a-committed docs/dev/* artifacts zero diff; (d) .gitignore zero diff.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Expired/missing sentinel falls through to legacy check_and_consume_allowlist (line 551-552). R2 verbatim says 'instead of grepping command text' but legacy path still grep-matches when no valid sentinel exists for the task AND a stale /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-${sid}.json grant file exists. AC1.2's verifiable criterion as worded ('orchestrator-authored pattern-in-comment NO LONGER satisfies the hook') IS still satisfied for the user's reported attack pattern: when no legacy grant exists, no bypass is possible (line 156: [ ! -f flag_file ] && return 1). Coexistence transition window is the residual concern.
out_of_scope_observations.follow_on_recommendation: Fully retire check_and_consume_allowlist legacy regex-pattern grant mechanism, OR make sentinel-failure suppress legacy path (extend SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 to cover SENTINEL_NONE-but-grant-file-exists).

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex flagged pretool/posttool race: two concurrent PreTool invocations for the same Bash command can both observe SENTINEL_OK before PostTool consumes the grant. Not the user's reported symptom (sequential pattern-stuffing via comment).
out_of_scope_observations.follow_on_recommendation: Atomically claim/rename/unlink the sentinel under flock in PreTool before returning allow, then let PostTool clean by tool-call id.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #4: comment-only attempt (sentinel exists + command does NOT structurally match) does not consume sentinel. AC2 invariant suppresses the legacy path on the comment-only attempt (line 525 SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1), so the attack itself is blocked, but the sentinel persists for a later valid use. Not the user's reported symptom.
out_of_scope_observations.follow_on_recommendation: Consume sentinel on PreTool deny when sentinel exists but command does not structurally match the comment-only pattern.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #5: 4 hook files exempted as behavioral_test_only:true (pretool-bash-safety.sh, posttool-allowlist-consume.py, userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh, stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh). Library functions ARE tested; hooks delegate to library. Integration tests would strengthen coverage but exemption is a reasonable engineering trade-off (hooks fire only via Claude Code harness).
out_of_scope_observations.follow_on_recommendation: Add checked-in integration tests invoking hooks with real JSON stdin payloads.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #1 (out_of_scope per Rule 3): Bash itself can write the sentinel JSON file at /tmp/claude-grants/<task>.json (no Bash write block for that path). User's reported attack was command-text comment stuffing; whether orchestrator can author the sentinel JSON via Bash is an adjacent attack vector outside the user's stated threat model. AC1.2's verifiable criterion empirically holds for the user's pattern. Adjacent defense-in-depth not part of /redev's scope.
out_of_scope_observations.follow_on_recommendation: Add safety layer blocking Bash writes to /tmp/claude-grants/ and make sentinel validation session-bound (task_id + session_id must match the current hook payload).

qa.overall_assessment: PASS. All 9 ACs empirically verified by direct file-content inspection, dev-created verifier scripts (final_sweep.sh emits 27 PASS lines), and QA-authored independent empirical tests (real-world close-report line 151 captured by AC5 regex; sentinel-grant matcher rejects comment-only and substring-in-echo attacks; push.sh rejects all 5 fixture types). 4 binding constraints fully preserved. No scope leak from prior task 20260519-161035. Codex consult returned 7 findings; per Rule 3 (caller filters), 1 classified out_of_scope (adjacent attack), 4 classified in_scope_minor (real defects but outside user's reported symptom — surfaced as follow-on observations), 1 N/A by AC10's 'OR if no playwright in cycle surface' carve-out, 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY confirming binding constraints. NO codex finding contradicts the user's verbatim requirement; all blocker-level codex findings are adjacent improvements per Rule 4 (requirement wins).

All 12 codex-using agents (architect, ba, dev, qa, pm, product-owner, spec, ui-specialist, user, style-inspector, cleanliness-inspector, prompt-inspector) have Skill in allowed_tools. Non-codex roles (rule-inspector, git-edge-case-analyst, cleaner, test-executor, test-validator) correctly lack Skill — verified each does NOT reference codex in its agent.md. policy_version bumped to 3, _note acknowledges reconciliation.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Bash-safety hook does NOT permit kill <pid> merely because command string contains literal /allow keyword. New sentinel-file mechanism requires separately-written grant.
success_criteria_results.criterion: AC2 R2 — sentinel-grant lifecycle replaces pattern matching
success_criteria_results.verification_method: (1) pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py -k terminal cases (2) QA-authored empirical Python test of match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() with 3 scenarios
success_criteria_results.details: pytest: 6 passed, 29 deselected. Empirical: attack 1 (comment '# /allow kill 123' + echo) → match=None; attack 2 ('echo "kill 123"' substring) → match=None; real 'kill 123' → match={op:kill, target:123}. Pattern-stuffing exploit closed via structural matching. AC2 invariant suppresses legacy /allow path when sentinel exists but command does NOT structurally match (pretool-bash-safety.sh:525 SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 → line 551 skips legacy).
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest 6 passed in 0.04s; Python heredoc test prints Attack1=None, Attack2=None, Real={dict}

Item 2 sentinel-grant replaces command-text-grep. Item 1 push.sh atomic abort prevents validation-bypass. Item 5 Step 7 4-stage algorithm prevents silent skip. Item 4 TTL bumped to 180s. Item 10 trap contract codified in agents/qa.md + ba.md (frozen spec untouched). Items 3, 6, 9 are 1-line doc reminders closing orchestrator-discipline gaps.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 7 findings (3 BLOCKER, 3 MAJOR, 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY) all targeting AC1.2 sentinel-grant security model and AC10 verification-coverage gaps. Per Rule 3 (caller filters codex output) and Rule 4 (requirement wins), 5 findings classified as out_of_scope_observations (adjacent attack vectors / coexistence-transition / race-window not in user's reported symptom). 1 finding addressed by AC10's 'OR N/A if no playwright in cycle surface' carve-out. 1 finding confirmed broader binding-constraint check passes. No codex finding contradicts a user-stated AC.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex #7 confirmation incorporated into regression_test_results (broader frozen-artifact check). Other 6 findings logged as out_of_scope_observations[] for orchestrator surface to user.

findings.title: Bash self-authors sentinel JSON file
findings.rationale: Adjacent attack vector — user's reported symptom was command-text comment-stuffing, NOT defense against orchestrator writing sentinel JSON via Bash. AC1.2 verifiable criterion satisfied for user's pattern. Defense-in-depth not requested.

findings.title: Expired/missing sentinel falls through to legacy /allow
findings.rationale: Real defect: R2 says 'instead of grepping command text' but legacy still runs when SENTINEL_NONE. HOWEVER: legacy requires pre-existing /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-${sid}.json (line 156 returns 1 if absent), so user's reported attack (pure comment-only with no prior grant) cannot succeed. Surfaced as follow-on for full legacy retirement.

findings.title: PreTool/PostTool race allows double-consume
findings.rationale: Real defect under concurrent Bash dispatch. Not user's sequential pattern-stuffing symptom. Surfaced as follow-on for atomic claim/rename in PreTool.

findings.title: Comment-only attempt doesn't consume sentinel
findings.rationale: AC2 invariant (line 525) BLOCKS the comment-only attack itself; sentinel persists for later valid use. Defensible behavior — not user's reported symptom.

findings.title: behavioral_test_only:true exemptions for 4 hook files
findings.rationale: Library functions ARE tested; hooks delegate to library. Integration tests would strengthen coverage. Surfaced as follow-on.

findings.title: AC10 template-only doesn't prove runtime cleanup
findings.rationale: AC10 verifiable text includes 'OR N/A if no playwright in this cycle's surface'. This cycle entirely file-content-grep — no playwright. Carve-out applies. Template landing per BA iter-3 scope reconciliation is the binding deliverable.

findings.title: Broader frozen-artifact check
findings.rationale: Confirmed: zero diff vs d988d4a for .gitignore, all 14 docs/dev/* artifacts, the spec file. Wrapper preserved. No 161035 scope leak. Incorporated into regression_test_results.
